name: The Sunken Choir
descriptionSeed: A pre-transition amphitheater half-drowned in a still black tarn below the Heights — tiered stone seats descending into water so quiet it doubles the sky. Before the transition it was built to hold sound the way a cup holds water; now, on certain nights, it still does. Stand at the rim and a chord you didn't sing comes back to you, changed. The Heights folk call it sacred and stay away; a few won't, and those few tend to come back different. This is not the contaminated river — the tarn is clear as glass, and what haunts it is older than the crisis and stranger.
encounterFlavor: A hush that isn't empty. Resonance that answers before it's made. Pilgrims who came to hear a dead voice and won't leave; a scholar mapping the echo; the sense that the Choir is patiently teaching itself to speak.
questSeeds: Vesper comes here every night to hear her drowned brother's voice in the return-chord and is slowly being unmade by it; someone could help her stop, or help her finish, and it isn't obvious which is mercy

The old Choirmaster believes the amphitheater is trying to complete a song interrupted at the transition, and that finishing it would either heal something or open something — he needs hands and doesn't fully care which
Sonic-attuned characters find the Choir will teach them, fast and free, in exchange for something it doesn't name up front — the price reveals itself only after the third lesson
A Heights family wants a relative's body recovered from the tarn before the Choir 'keeps' the voice for good; diving the drowned tiers is its own peril

substrateSource.reason: A pre-Transition sonic work, flooded and intact. The water preserved the lattice as surely as it preserved the stone.
substrateSource.radiusNote: `radius` is in legacy map units; `radiusWorld` is RADII on the sphere and is the one mechanics should use. Chosen by sweep against the authored worldPos: at this scale all 26 sources resolve on the correct side of their region baseline and calibration drift stays ~0.05. Larger radii blanket a whole region (a source at ~55% strength everywhere inside it) and renormalization cancels the field flat — the failure CCode measured and reverted.

axisVectorNote: Derived from authored poleIntensity against world_node_atlas.axisOrder (SNG-180). Disposition — what this place IS. Distinct from world position.
worldPosNote: A pre-Transition sonic work, flooded. Valley country, below the water table. (SNG-180: authored position — WHERE it sits. Independent of axisVector, which is WHAT it is; a bastion is where the two disagree.)
